# SEG800- Workshop 3

Mark and submission files

Please work in **<u>groups</u>** to complete this lab.

This lab is worth 3% of the total course grade and will be evaluated
through your written submission, as well as the lab demo/test. During
the lab demo, group members are *randomly* selected to explain the
submitted solution. Group members absent during the lab demo will lose
the demo mark.

## **Learning Objectives**

- Evaluate z-transforms of signals and system responses using definition
  and properties of z-transform.

- Determine the ROC of z-transforms

- Use MATLAB functions to generate and plot the pole-zero plots of
  signals and systems.

## **Preparation**

- This workshop assumes you have already installed MATLAB and downloaded
  the DSP Software Package in workshop 1.

- This workshop uses the DSP Student Manual **\[DSP-SM\]** available at:

> <https://media.pearsoncmg.com/ph/esm/ecs_proakis_dsp_5/stu_assets/dsp_student_manual.pdf>
>
> Use solutions in Appendix B as reference when needed.

- Review \[DSP-SM\] Section 3.1 to 3.4.

- Review the functions listed in \[DSP-SM\] Section 3.5.

- Create a live script in file **WS03.mlx**. Include text and code to
  complete this workshop. When asked to prove or calculate analytically,
  feel free to include a snapshot of your handwritten solutions in a
  separate document if easier. Please organize your solutions.

## **Part I: The Z-transform and its properties**

Solve the following problems. Include solutions and explanations in your
script/ submission.

1.  \[DSP-SM\] P3.1

2.  \[DSP-SM\] P3.2

3.  \[DSP-SM\] P3.3

4.  \[DSP-SM\] P3.4

5.  Include solutions (with explanations) to the following problem:

> \[DSP\] P 3.1 Determine the z-transform of the following signals.
>
> <img src="data:image/png;base64,"
> style="width:2.72277in;height:1.51006in"
> alt="A math equations and numbers Description automatically generated with medium confidence" />

6.  Include solutions (with explanations) to the following problem:

> \[DSP\] P 3.2 Determine the z-transforms of the following signals and
> sketch the corresponding pole–zero patterns.
>
> a\. $x(n) = (1 + n)\ u(n)$
>
> e\. $x(n) = \ \left( na^{n}\cos{\omega_{0}n} \right)\ u(n)$
>
> h\.
> $x(n) = \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)^{n}\left\lbrack u(n) - u(n - 10) \right\rbrack$

7.  Include solutions (with explanations) to the following problem:

> \[DSP\] P 3.3 Determine the z-transforms and sketch the ROC of the
> following signals.
>
> <img src="data:image/png;base64,"
> style="width:2.90016in;height:1.97193in"
> alt="A math equations on a white background Description automatically generated" />

## **Part II: The inverse z-transform**

Solve the following problems. Include solutions and explanations in your
script/ submission.

1.  \[DSP-SM\] P3.5

2.  Include <u>analytical</u> solutions (with explanations) to the
    following problem:

\[DSP\] P 3.14 Determine the causal signal x(n) if its z-transform X(z)
is given by:

> a\. $X(z) = \ \frac{1 + 3z^{- 1}}{1 + 3z^{- 1} + 2z^{- 2}}$
>
> c\. $X(z) = \ \frac{z^{- 6} + z^{- 7}}{1 - z^{- 1}}$
>
> h\. X(z) is specified by a pole-zero pattern in Fig. P3.14. The
> constat G = ½.
>
> <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"
> style="width:2in;height:1.53482in"
> alt="The figure shows a pole-zero plot. For long description in Notes pane, press F6." />
>
> Figure P3.14

## **Part III: Analysis of LTI Systems**

Solve the following problems. Include solutions and explanations in your
script/ submission.

3.  \[DSP-SM\] P3.7

4.  \[DSP-SM\] P3.8

5.  Include <u>analytical</u> solutions (with explanations) to the
    following problem:

\[DSP\] P 3.35 Compute the zero-state response for the following pairs
of systems and input signals.

h\.
$h(n) = \ \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)^{n}u(n),\ \ \ x(n) = (n + 1)\left( \frac{1}{4} \right)^{n}u(n)$

6.  Include solutions (with explanations) to the following problem:

\[DSP\] P 3.37 Compute the response of the system

$$y(n) = 0.7y(n - 1) - 0.12y(n - 2) + x(n - 1) + x(n - 2)$$

to the input *x(n) = n u(n)*. Is the system stable?

## **Part IV: Group work**

Complete this declaration by adding your names:

> We, <span class="mark">------------ (mention your names),</span>
> declare that the attached assignment is our own work in accordance
> with the Seneca Academic Policy. We have not copied any part of this
> assignment, manually or electronically, from any other source
> including web sites, unless specified as references. We have not
> distributed our work to other students.

Specify what each member has done towards the completion of this work:

|     | Name | Task(s) |
|-----|------|---------|
| 1   |      |         |
| 2   |      |         |
| 3   |      |         |

Team members and tasks

Include above in a separate document or in your MATLAB script for
submission.

## **Submission**

Run your live script and export to pdf. Include the pdf in your
submission.

Please submit the submission file(s) through Blackboard. Only the
<u>last submission</u> will be marked. Only one person must submit for
the group
